medications: Naltrexone 50 mg daily (for alcohol use disorder)
health.mental_health: In sustained early recovery from alcohol use disorder; attends support meetings, has periods of anxiety and guilt about past behavior, and is working on sleep hygiene and coping skills to prevent relapse.
recent_visits: Primary care follow-up at Massachusetts General Hospital for blood pressure management (last month)
Urology appointment at Brigham and Women's Hospital for urinary symptoms and PSA monitoring (2 months ago)
Behavioral health intake at a community health center in Dorchester for addiction recovery support (3 months ago)
Lab work (lipids, CMP, A1c) at Quest Diagnostics in Cambridge (6 weeks ago)

finance.debt: Carries about $7,800 on credit cards from a period of missed work and legal/administrative expenses tied to past drinking; on a hardship repayment plan and trying to rebuild credit.
investments: Small 401(k) from a previous warehouse job (no longer contributing)
High-yield savings account (emergency fund goal: 3 months expenses)
I Bonds (small amount purchased during high inflation)
recent_transactions: Monthly rent payment for a studio in Jamaica Plain
Car payment and rideshare insurance premium
Prescription copays (naltrexone, blood pressure meds, tamsulosin)
Payment to a credit card on a negotiated plan
MBTA CharlieCard auto-reload and occasional commuter rail tickets
Donation to an AA group basket and purchase of recovery literature
Vehicle maintenance: new tires and oil change at a shop in Roxbury

relationships: Ex-wife: Linda Carver (limited contact; rebuilding trust through consistent check-ins)
Adult daughter: Emily Carver (lives in Somerville; cautiously reconnecting, occasional dinners)
Adult son: Jason Carver (lives out of state; mostly phone calls, relationship strained)
Sister: Mei Liu (lives in Quincy; supportive, helps with appointments when needed)
Sponsor: Tom R. (met through AA; regular accountability calls)
social.social_media_activity: Low activity; has a Facebook account mainly to follow local Boston neighborhood updates and family photos, rarely posts and keeps most settings private.
private_conversations: Apologies and boundary-setting conversations with his ex-wife and daughter about past drinking and broken commitments
Discussions with sponsor about cravings, triggers, and relapse prevention after stressful shifts
Conversations with his sister about health worries (blood pressure spikes, urinary symptoms) and fear of cancer screening results

schedule.work_hours: Typically 45–55 hours/week driving; weekdays 6:00–11:00 AM and 3:00–8:00 PM, plus a Saturday evening shift; avoids late-night bar close when possible to reduce relapse triggers.
regular_appointments: AA meeting (in-person) Tuesday and Thursday at 7:00 PM in Back Bay
Telehealth therapy session every other Monday at 5:30 PM
Blood pressure check at a local CVS MinuteClinic once a month
upcoming_events: Annual physical scheduled next Wednesday morning
Coffee with daughter in Davis Square this Sunday
Car inspection due by the end of the month
Court-ordered driver safety refresher course (online) next Friday afternoon (related to an older incident)
schedule.daily_routine: Wakes around 5:00 AM, checks BP, drinks coffee, starts driving early commuter routes; takes a midday break for a walk on the Emerald Necklace or a nap; returns for afternoon/evening demand; ends the day with a brief check-in call to sponsor or journaling before bed.

professional.company: Independent contractor driving primarily for Uber and Lyft in the Greater Boston area
projects: Maintaining a 4.9+ rating by improving cleanliness and consistent pickup communication
Tracking mileage and expenses weekly for quarterly estimated taxes
Testing safer, lower-stress routes (airport runs and medical center pickups) to avoid nightlife triggers
Saving toward a hybrid vehicle to reduce fuel and maintenance costs
professional.performance_issues: Has a prior alcohol-related driving suspension on record from years ago that still affects insurance costs and causes anxiety during background check renewals; also occasionally declines high-risk late-night pickups to protect sobriety, which can reduce earnings.
workplace_relationships: Friendly network of drivers who meet near Logan Airport staging lots for tips on surge patterns
Occasional tension with a local dispatcher-style driver group chat when he refuses late-night bar pickups
Relies on a preferred mechanic in Roxbury and a detailer in South Boston to keep the car rideshare-ready

hobbies: Long walks along the Charles River Esplanade
Listening to jazz and public radio while driving
Cooking simple Chinese-American comfort meals (stir-fries, soups)
Reading history books from the Boston Public Library
Weekend morning visits to the Boston Public Market
habits: Tracks blood pressure at home and limits caffeine after noon
Avoids bars and keeps non-alcoholic seltzer in the car to manage cravings
Journals nightly about triggers and gratitude in recovery
Uses a pill organizer to stay consistent with medications
Occasionally stress-eats salty snacks during long shifts (working to reduce for hypertension)
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives alone in a small rented studio in Jamaica Plain; keeping a stable, quiet home environment is part of his recovery plan, and he’s slowly allowing family to visit again.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Trying to follow a lower-sodium, heart-healthy diet; prefers home-cooked meals, limits red meat, and avoids alcohol entirely; struggles with late-night convenience food when shifts run long.
